er makes for his character in the Life Path_ as well as certain Life Path events_ will determine the balance of character" $ skills_ After completing the Life Path_ the player can add few extra skills t0 round out his 0r her character. Where path grants skill; the numbers listed after rep resent the Skill Points the character earns toward advancement that Skill: negative number decreases the number of Skill Points earned for that skill, to minimum of Skill Point once that skill is awarded path awards points toward any skill; the   player can add those points any skill the character already has_ but not to new Skill Upon finishing the Life Path, the player adds Up the totab points earned for each skill to find its skill bonus using the Skill Points Table (p. 57). The number of Skill Points earned in the Life Path should not be confused with Skill Bonuses granted for Skill advance: ment (see the Character Creation Worksheet; p. 218).
For subskills listed as Any, the player may cnoose any sub- skill, appropriate his character'$ affiliation and back ground; For example, character granted Interest /Any Skill mignt choose take Interest /Hiking perhaps Interest/Science Fiction_ You can add these Skill Points to a Any Subskill of the same type whether it is one your character has already earned or a entirely new subskill For example player who chose Interest/Hiking receives another Interest / Any Skill; he can add those Skill Points t0 the Hiking Skill or use them for new Interest Skiii:
Occupational Fields Most professions entail certain skills_ Mw3 arranges these sets of skills in occupational fields. Each field consists of sev- eral skills related to particular occupation; The most common fields for MechWarrior characters are shown starting on 52. Fields are used in the Life Path to simplify the assignment of skills For example; rather than listing every Basic Training Skill every time character earns one the path simply awards the Basic  Training Field When field is awarded througn the Life Path: the character receives Skill Points toward every skiii that field. The character gets these points every time the field is awarded even the character repeats path:
SkIlL BONUSES skill bonus represents character' = degree of ability in particular skill. This number is applied to the die roll result for any Action Check involving that skill_ Beginners in skill have +0 Skill Bonus_ Low as that is_ they are still better at the skill than characters who do not have it at all (see Untrained Skill Use; p. 16). As character" abili- ty increases so does or her skill bonus_ Characters can have skill bonuses as high as +10, though few starting charac ters display even half this level of ability.
EVENTS Everybody grows up. Many kids in the thirty-first century grow up on the streets_ But how many street kids were taken group of mercenaries and taught how t0 drive tank? These sorts unusual events make each   character' $ story unique and are the source of unusual skills and traits that might not result from the standard Life Path_ Each path listing includes table of special events keyed t0 the appropriate cir: cumstances_ which reinforce the character' background each step of his life_ Unlike the other parts of character creation, events are determined   randomly rolling   206  (this roll not open- ended) . Roll once on the event table for each path or subpath your character takes taking= subpath_ use the event table for the main Dath under which the subpath iS given your Charac ter takes single path multiple times follow the special rules for repeating paths see Repeating Paths_ p. 251.
BUBSKILLS Some skills actually represent groups of related subskills_ For example character cannot   simply have the   Technician Skill but must possess particular aspect Technician /Electronics_ Technician/Lasers and so on_ Certain skills have specific Jists of subskills_ while others have variety depending on the desires of the players and gamemaster: For  example the Arts Skill covers kinds of artistic expression from watercolor painting to clay sculpture t0 zero-G dancing: All the possibilities do not appear on the Skill List (p. 58) because there are simply too many: Instead_ the player (or the Life Path) defines exactly what Arts Subskillls) character has. Each skill that consists of subskills IS defined in the individual skill description (beginning on 95) and marked with an asterisk (* on the Skill List For purposes of game play; every subskill should be treat- distinct skill. For example; your character has Technician / Electronics and Technician/Lasers he or she uses and advances each one separately: In the Life Path, some subskills are listed as affiliation: These  skills are related to the character' chosen affiliation (see 26). For example. character   from the Capellan Confederation who gains the Academic /Affiliation History Skill actually has the Academic /Capellan Confederation History Skill. character receives Language /Affiliation, that skill may rep resent any primary or secondary language used by that charac ter' affiliation_
Rolling Boxcars On an event roll result of 12 (double sixes). YOU can cnoose any event on the table (if somelning ooks good for your char- acter) Or roll twice for two random events you prefer lots of random chance). If you choose to roll twice and roll another 12, ignore that result and roll again until you get different result:
Event Options Generating random events adds a element of uncertainty to the character creation process It can be fun have the unexpected occur in your character's life_ and even unpleasant unlucky events are guaranteed add  interesting   flavor: However; the players and gamemaster agree_ you have few
